title: the-loop CLI configuration
description: Schema for the-loop's CLI config (cli-config.yaml: webhooks/polling/eventLog), read by `the-loop gh-webhook`/`poll`/`sessions`/`events`. Independent of .the-loop/config.schema.json (the per-repo PLUGIN config): the CLI is expected to work across multiple repos, so its config resolves in priority order: --config/-c flag, $THE_LOOP_CLI_CONFIG env var, ./.the-loop/cli-config.yaml (repo-relative — an operator may choose to track it in a specific repo), else ~/.the-loop/cli-config.yaml. See decision-032.

webhooks.description: Config for the the-loop CLI webhook receiver(s) (e.g. `the-loop gh-webhook start`).

secretEnv.description: Env var holding the GitHub webhook secret for HMAC verification (never stored in config).

events.description: GitHub event names of interest, e.g. pull_request_review_comment, workflow_run. Empty = accept all event types.

routing.description: Route received events to registered harness sessions via the session registry (docs/specs/issue-15/design.md, decision-016).

registryDir.description: Directory of per-session registry JSON files (git-ignored).

defaultHarness.description: Harness used when spawning a session for an unmatched event.

spawnOnUnmatched.description: Policy for events matching no registered session: 'never' log-and-drop; 'always' spawn+register a new session; 'labeled' spawn only when the issue/PR carries autoExecuteLabel.

autoExecuteLabel.description: Issue/PR label that opts a work item into autonomous execution (spawnOnUnmatched: labeled). Read from the webhook payload, no API call.

authorizedUsers.description: SECURITY (prompt-injection guard): GitHub logins whose actions the-loop may act on. Comments/reviews/labels/items authored by anyone not listed are ignored by BOTH the webhook receiver and the poller. Empty = fall back to ticketing.github.owner in the PLUGIN config (.the-loop/config.yaml) of the repo the daemon happens to be started from, if any; if that is also unset, human-authored events are all ignored (fail closed). Each operator runs their own instance for their own logins.

spawnWorkdir.description: Working directory for sessions spawned on unmatched events.

runner.description: How spawned sessions are hosted (receiver-global): 'process' = headless one-shot subprocess; 'tmux' = interactive harness TUI in a named tmux session (loop-<slug>) humans can attach to (issue-32, decision-021).

webTerminal.description: Optional ttyd-served browser terminal onto the tmux sessions (runner: tmux). No auth of its own — access control is environmental: localhost, VPN, or the hosting provider's network (decision-021).

enabled.description: Serve tmux sessions over HTTP via ttyd (verified at receiver start).

host.description: Interface/IP ttyd binds. Keep 127.0.0.1 unless the network layer protects wider exposure.

maxConcurrentDispatches.description: Cap on harness dispatches running in parallel (per-session dispatch is always serialized).

dedupCacheSize.description: Bounded LRU of X-GitHub-Delivery ids for at-most-once processing.

dispatchTimeoutSeconds.description: Timeout for a single harness resume/spawn subprocess.

promptTemplate.description: string.Template rendered into the prompt that resumes a session. the-loop's internal template; the dispatcher falls back to a built-in default when the path is absent (the case in a project repo, which does not carry the-loop's templates). Set a repo-relative path to override.

spawnPromptTemplate.description: string.Template rendered into the prompt for a newly spawned (auto-execute) session — kicks off the-loop's work-on flow. the-loop's internal template; the dispatcher falls back to a built-in default when the path is absent. Set a repo-relative path to override.

harnessArgs.description: Extra CLI args per harness (e.g. permission flags). The dispatcher never widens permissions itself.

polling.description: Config for the the-loop CLI poller (`the-loop poll start`) — a pull-based, provider-agnostic alternative to the webhook receiver for hosts a webhook cannot reach. Each entry in `sources` selects a provider (e.g. github) the system interfaces with; the core carries no provider-specific knobs. Dispatch behaviour (harness, runner, spawn policy, templates, registry) is reused from `webhooks.ghWebhook.routing`.

intervalSeconds.description: Seconds between poll cycles (applies to all sources).

stateFile.description: Durable JSON tracking which comments each item has processed (cross-poll/restart dedup). Git-ignored runtime state.

sources.description: Ordered list of provider-specific poll sources. Each entry MUST name a `provider`; the remaining keys are that provider's own config (unknown to the core).

provider.description: Which poll provider handles this source. Only `github` ships today (jira is reserved for a follow-up).

label.description: Label gating what this source polls. Empty = reuse webhooks.ghWebhook.routing.autoExecuteLabel.

repos.description: [github] Repositories to poll as OWNER/REPO. Empty = fall back to ticketing.github in the PLUGIN config (.the-loop/config.yaml) of the repo the daemon happens to be started from, if any — set this explicitly for a daemon watching repos other than the one it runs from.

ghBinary.description: [github] Path/name of the gh CLI (uses the user's existing gh auth).

eventLog.description: Structured JSONL event log of every routing/dispatch/session decision made by the-loop's CLI processes (gh-webhook, poll, sessions). Queried with `the-loop events`; schema in skills/the-loop/reference/observability.md (decision-025). Top-level here (not nested under an `observability` key) — the CLI config has no dev/runtime log-level concept, that stays with the PLUGIN config's `observability.devLevel`/`runtimeLevel`/`browserLogging`.
